skill.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
skill.description: Sets up an Agent skills section and docs/agents configuration so Matt Pocock's engineering skills know the repository issue tracker, triage labels, and domain documentation layout.
skill.summary: Configures repository context for Matt Pocock's engineering skill suite.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the confirmed matches are Markdown inline code, CLI examples, and documentation links rather than executable malware. The skill is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it asks an agent to inspect repository files, run issue-tracker CLIs, and write configuration files after user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Repository Configuration File Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to add or update an Agent skills section in CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md and to write docs/agents configuration files. This is legitimate setup behavior, but it can alter project guidance files and should be reviewed by the user before writing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is explicitly documented in the skill process and write sections. The risk is capability-based, not malicious, because the skill requires presenting a draft and getting user confirmation first.

medium_findings.title: Issue Tracker CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill configures downstream workflows that use gh or glab commands to create, read, comment on, label, and close issues. These commands are expected for an issue-tracker setup skill, but they can publish data to external services when used later.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly document git, gh, and glab command usage. The commands are standard issue-tracker operations, so the concern is external side effects rather than command injection.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution across the Markdown files. These are inline code spans and fenced examples that document filenames, labels, and CLI commands; they are not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All affected files are Markdown guidance files, and the backticks render code text for readers. No script file, interpreter directive, or executable command wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators in Markdown text. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, cipher configuration, hashing code, or dependency usage in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain prose and Markdown code formatting, not cryptographic APIs. The repository contains only Markdown files for this skill, so the high-severity crypto finding is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Is Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged hardcoded URLs at the GitLab CLI references. These links point to GitLab CLI documentation and do not send repository data, credentials, or telemetry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears as a Markdown hyperlink identifying the glab CLI. No network request code or data payload appears at either cited location.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents gh, glab, and git command use for repository and issue-tracker setup. These are not executed by bundled code, but they are external commands the agent may run during the workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly listed as workflow instructions. They are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they are still external command patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Configuration File Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to update AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md and generate docs/agents files. This is expected behavior, but marketplace users should know the skill can change repository guidance files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write targets and selection rules are directly specified in the skill. The instructions include user review before writing, which lowers malicious-risk confidence but not capability confidence.

content.value_statement: Matt Pocock's engineering skills need repository-specific context before they can manage issues, triage labels, and domain documentation. This skill guides setup and writes the local configuration files those skills read.
actual_capabilities: Checks repository context such as git remotes, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md, and docs/agents.
Guides users through issue tracker selection for GitHub, GitLab, local Markdown, or another workflow.
Maps five canonical triage roles to the label strings used by the repository.
Records whether the project uses a single-context or multi-context domain documentation layout.
Drafts and writes docs/agents configuration files after user review.
Updates an existing Agent skills section instead of appending a duplicate.
limitations: It configures only Matt Pocock engineering skills, not unrelated skill collections.
It requires user decisions and should not be treated as a fully automated installer.
It assumes gh or glab is installed and authenticated when GitHub or GitLab workflows are used.
It does not migrate existing issues, labels, or domain documentation.

use_cases.title: Prepare a new repository
use_cases.description: Set up issue tracker, triage, and domain documentation conventions before using the related engineering skills.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers adopting Matt Pocock skills

use_cases.title: Standardize team workflow
use_cases.description: Create shared docs/agents configuration so team members and AI assistants use the same repository conventions.

use_cases.title: Repair missing skill context
use_cases.description: Refresh the configuration when issue tracker details, label names, or domain documentation layout have changed.

prompt_templates.title: Run first setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use setup-matt-pocock-skills to configure this repository for Matt Pocock engineering skills.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a repository that has not used this skill suite before.

prompt_templates.title: Review existing setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use setup-matt-pocock-skills to inspect the current Agent skills configuration and show what is missing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirming the current state before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Customize labels
prompt_templates.prompt: Use setup-matt-pocock-skills and map the triage roles to our existing issue labels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adapting the workflow to a team that already has label names.

prompt_templates.title: Configure a custom tracker
prompt_templates.prompt: Use setup-matt-pocock-skills and document our non-GitHub issue tracker workflow in docs/agents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup for Jira, Linear, or another issue management process.

output_examples.input: Configure this repository for Matt Pocock engineering skills.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the detected GitHub remote, confirms default triage labels, drafts the Agent skills section, and writes the docs/agents files after approval.

output_examples.input: Show me whether this repo is already configured.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes whether AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md has an Agent skills section and lists any missing docs/agents configuration files.

output_examples.input: Set this up for our custom issue tracker.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for the workflow, records the issue publishing and lookup rules, and saves them as local agent documentation.

best_practices: Run the skill before using to-issues, triage, to-prd, diagnose, tdd, or architecture skills.
Review the draft Agent skills section and docs/agents files before approving writes.
Keep the generated files in version control so the team shares the same conventions.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill without checking which repository guidance file it will edit.
Do not choose GitHub or GitLab unless the matching CLI is installed and authenticated.
Do not use default triage labels if the repository already has different label names.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates an Agent skills section and writes docs/agents files for issue tracking, triage labels, and domain docs.

faq.question: Does it support GitLab?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a GitLab template that uses the glab CLI and GitLab issue terminology.

faq.question: Can it use local Markdown issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can document a local .scratch workflow for repositories that do not use a hosted issue tracker.

faq.question: Will it overwrite existing AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md content?
faq.answer: It is instructed to update the existing Agent skills section in place and preserve surrounding user content.

faq.question: Does it make network requests by itself?
faq.answer: No bundled code makes requests. The documented GitHub and GitLab workflows use external CLIs that contact those services.

faq.question: When should I rerun it?
faq.answer: Rerun it when switching issue trackers, changing triage label vocabulary, or resetting the generated setup files.
